title: membership queries in(&g)/groupsOf/statesIn
manager: Runtime queries over group membership — `in(&g)` (is the current state in this group), `groupsOf(s)` (which groups contain s), `statesIn(&g)` (the group's members) — so guards and logic can ask about hierarchy directly. `in(&g)` in particular becomes a guard predicate, letting a transition depend on "are we anywhere in the active region," and it later feeds the temporal properties of the proofs era.
cs: in(&g)/groupsOf/statesIn query API; in(&g) usable as a guard predicate (W6.61); feeds era-3 properties; fsl#1343 in-state lineage.
